mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where UNRATE > 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail auto sales combined with low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer credit expansion and durable goods demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 5% YoY while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during post-shock recovery phases, or the 5-day return distribution shows no significant deviation from zero (t-stat < 1.5) when INDPRO growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend indicates expanding economic output and corporate earnings capacity, which systematically generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.4%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms synchronized labor and construction sector expansion, which drives broad-based corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k and HOUST exceeds 1.1M, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output and profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to achieve a Rank IC > 0.03 during contraction phases.
